project.title: Role of RNA binding protein PgRBP1 in alteration of protein profile of Porphyromonas gingivalis
project.projectDescription: The oral periodontal pathogen Porphyromonas gingivalis must adapt to ever changing environment to survive and cause disease.  So far most of the efforts concerning the regulatory mechanisms employed by the bacterium centered on DNA-binding regulators.  Here we focused on a RNA-binding protein that is indispensable for the bacterium to survive with host cells.  We have determined the proteome affected by deletion of the protein, designated here PgRBP1. Three strains were used: WT, PgRBP1-deficient strain designated V3139 and a complemented strain V3236.  In total, 1,393 proteins were identified. 592 proteins in the comparison of P. gingivalis 3139 vs. WT, 228 proteins in the comparison of P. gingivalis 3236 vs. WT, and 276 proteins in the comparison of P. gingivalis 3139 vs. 3236 showed differential level with statistical significance
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Bacterial Growth: Porphyromonas gingivalis W83, V3139 (∆PgRBP1), and V 3236 (V3139 with restored RBP1) were maintained on blood agar plates at 37oC in the anaerobic chamber in artificial atmosphere composed of 80% N, 10% H, and 10% CO2. BHI broth cultures were prepared and grown until  logarithmic phase. Bacterial cultures that reached an OD600 of 0.7 were pelleted and adjusted in PBS buffer to 10OD/ml. Samples were frozen in -20°C before sending for proteomics analysis.    • The Quantitative total proteome analysis using TMT method was used for protein quantification. Sample was prepared by lysing the bacteria in a lysis buffer composed of: 8 M urea/50 mM tetraethyl ammoniumbicarbonate. Proteins were digested with LysC (LysC to protein ratio of 1:100) and trypsin (trypsin to protein ratio of 1:100). Labeling method used was 10-plex TMT. Peptides were fractionated with basic pH RPLC. No of fractions: 12 Mass spectrometry analysis: Mass spectrometry analysis instrument: Orbitrap Fusion Lumos (Thermo Scientific) – On-line liquid chromatography: Easy nLC 1200 (Thermo) – 2 h running time per fraction – MS1 resolution: 120,000 – MS2 resolution: 30,000 – Fragmentation method: HCD – Collision energy for MS2: 32
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Database Search was as follows: Database search software: Proteome Discoverer 2.1. Database search parameters: – Database used: Porphyromonas gingivalis (strain W83) UniProt (released on Apr 2017) – 2 missed cleavages were allowed – Cleavage enzyme: trypsin – Minimum AA length: 6 – Minimum peptides for protein: 1  – Fixed modification: Carbamidomethyl on cysteine residue and TMT on Lys and peptide N-terminal – Dynamic modifications:  Oxidation on M, and acetyl on N-terminus – 1% false discovery rate was applied for both peptide and protein levels –  Statistical analysis was as follows:  • Analysis software: Perseus (BMC Bioinformatics. 2012;13 Suppl 16:S12. doi: 10.1186/1471-2105-13-S16-S12. Epub 2012 Nov 5.) • p-value: calculated by Student’s t-test • q-value: calculated by significance analysis of microarrays (SAM) and permutation based false discovery rate (FDR). • s0 value for SAM: 0.1   • Reference for SAM:  Tusher, Tibshirani and Chu (2001) PNAS 98, pp5116-21

references.referenceLine: Yanamandra S, Marsh H, Cvitkovic R, Gui Q, Belvin BR, Lewis JP. The Porphyromonas gingivalis RNA-binding protein is required for growth in high levels of zinc and persistence with host cells. Front Cell Infect Microbiol. 2025 15:1569544
